contents:
# Chairmen Garbarino and Moolenaar Launch Joint Probe into PRC AI National Security Risks

## Overview
House Committee on Homeland Security Chairman Andrew R. Garbarino and House Select Committee on China Chairman John Moolenaar announced a joint investigation into national security and cybersecurity risks from PRC-developed AI models. The probe targets low-cost, open-weight, and API-accessible systems by Chinese firms like DeepSeek, Alibaba, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax. As an initial step, they sent letters to Anysphere and Airbnb about risks from using PRC AI.[1]

## Details
The letters cite a White House Office of Science and Technology Policy memo from April 2026 warning of Chinese entities distilling U.S. AI systems via proxy accounts. Anysphere's Cursor Composer 2 model remained built on Moonshot AI's open-weight model implicated in distillation campaigns. This follows prior letters to Anthropic, Google, and Quantum Xchange after Anthropic's report on a PRC state-sponsored cyber actor using its AI for an autonomous cyberattack. The investigation addresses risks like disinformation and cyber defense weakening.[1]

**Location**: Washington, D.C., United States

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @SchizointReL, which covers intelligence operations, cyber warfare, disinformation. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following intelligence operations will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
